sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Disney, other U.S. companies offer abortion travel benefit after Roe decision
feed.summary: Following the U.S. Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade, numerous U.S. companies, including Walt Disney Co. and Meta Platforms Inc., announced they would cover travel expenses for employees seeking abortion services. This move comes as many states are expected to restrict or ban abortions, making it necessary for employees to travel to states where the procedure remains legal. Companies like Dick's Sporting Goods, Lyft, Yelp, Microsoft, Tesla, Apple, Alaska Air Group, Johnson & Johnson, OkCupid, Bumble, Netflix, and JPMorgan Chase & Co. are also offering similar benefits or legal protections.

feed.title: Apple Readies iPhone 14 and HomePod Upgrade in Flood of New Products
feed.summary: Apple is preparing for a busy period in late 2022 and early 2023, with plans to release new products including the iPhone 14 and an updated HomePod. This follows the WWDC software releases, ongoing developments like a US Apple store unionizing, and new hires. The company's revamped CarPlay is also signaling future advancements.

feed.title: T-Mobile is now selling data on which apps you open
feed.summary: T-Mobile has launched its new "App Insights" program, which sells app usage data to advertisers, allowing them to target users based on the apps installed and engagement patterns. While the program focuses on Android devices, Apple's strict privacy guidelines largely protect iPhone users from data collection, though T-Mobile notes that it is "kicking the tires" on incorporating iOS data in the future. Android users can opt out of the program through the "Magenta Marketing Platform Choices" app.

feed.title: Abortion assistance: Kroger to offer up to $4,000 of out-of-state travel costs for employees seeking abortion
feed.summary: Kroger announced it will offer up to $4,000 to cover out-of-state travel costs for employees seeking abortion and fertility treatments, following the Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade. The company, headquartered in Cincinnati, operates stores in states like Ohio and Kentucky where abortion is now banned or mostly banned. This move aligns Kroger with nearly two dozen other major U.S. companies providing similar assistance.

feed.title: Target completes 1,000th store remodel — here’s what it means
feed.summary: Target Corp. has completed its 1,000th store remodel, a key milestone in its "stores-first" strategy, which has shown a 2% to 4% sales bump in updated stores. The company's 2022 remodel program is its most ambitious yet, including full-store remodels, fulfillment remodels to boost same-day services, and the addition of Drive Up canopies. These renovations incorporate welcoming elements, consistent layouts, and shop-in-shop experiences, while also focusing on sustainability and improving customer loyalty.

feed.title: Rashida Jones and Will McCormack Sign First Look Deal at Lionsgate TV, Develop ‘Lovesick’ Adaptation
feed.summary: Rashida Jones and Will McCormack's production company, Le Train Train, has signed a first-look deal with Lionsgate Television Group to produce scripted projects across various platforms. Their first project under the deal is an adaptation of the UK romantic comedy series "Lovesick." Jones and McCormack will executive produce the adaptation, and a writer is currently being sought.

feed.title: Watch: When Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer laughed at iPhone
feed.summary: This article revisits a famous incident where then-Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer publicly dismissed the original Apple iPhone upon its launch. He criticized its high price and lack of a physical keyboard, believing it wouldn't appeal to business users. Years later, Ballmer admitted his mistake, acknowledging Apple's innovative business model and regretting Microsoft's slow entry into the hardware business for mobile devices.

feed.title: BP, Shell To Pay Employee Travel Costs After Abortion Ruling
feed.summary: Following the U.S. Supreme Court's reversal of Roe v. Wade, BP Plc and Shell Plc announced they would cover travel expenses for U.S. employees needing to access health services not available near their homes. This makes them the first major oil companies to implement such policies. Both companies maintain significant operations in Texas, a state expected to ban or severely restrict abortions, joining other corporate giants in expanding employee health benefits.

feed.title: Apple hikes Japan price of iPhone by nearly a fifth
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has increased the price of its flagship iPhone in Japan by nearly 20% due to the weakening yen currency and rising inflation. The entry-level iPhone 13 now costs 117,800 yen ($870), up from 99,800 yen previously. This price hike comes as Japanese consumers are already facing increased costs for daily necessities.

feed.title: Xfinity Stream app Launches on Apple TV 4K and Apple TV HD
feed.summary: Comcast has launched the Xfinity Stream app on Apple TV 4K and Apple TV HD, providing Xfinity TV customers with a new way to access their content within their homes. The app introduces a redesigned, intuitive user interface focused on content discovery, including editorial recommendations and personalization. This initiative follows a previous collaboration between Comcast and Apple to bring the Apple TV+ app to Comcast's entertainment platforms.

feed.title: Apple Ex-Corporate Law Chief Admits Years of Insider Trading
authors: Chris Dolmetsch and Bob Van Voris
feed.summary: Gene Levoff, Apple's former director of corporate law and co-chairman of its disclosure committee, pleaded guilty to six counts of securities fraud. He admitted to using his access to unreleased financial information from 2011 to 2016 to personally profit through insider trading. Levoff was ironically responsible for policing insider trading at the company.

feed.title: The Apple-1 Computer, a Vintage Tech Grail That Ignited the World of Personal Computing
feed.summary: The article discusses the Apple-1 computer, a rare vintage tech item that played a pivotal role in the inception of personal computing. Only about 82 of the original 200 units are believed to still exist, with one recently selling for approximately $340,000 on eBay. The device, which required users to provide a power supply, keyboard, storage, and display, was groundbreaking for offering a complete circuit board during a time when most computers were sold as DIY kits.

feed.title: What are FAANG stocks?
feed.summary: FAANG is an acronym for five leading technology companies: Facebook (now Meta), Amazon, Apple, Netflix, and Google (now Alphabet). These companies are known for their consistent growth, innovation, and significant impact on the American stock market, particularly the NASDAQ 100 and S&P 500 indices. While they have shown strong performance and market capitalization, there's an ongoing debate among investors about their valuation.

feed.title: Delta reportedly paid passengers $10K to leave oversold flight
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines reportedly offered passengers up to $10,000 each to volunteer to leave an overbooked flight from Michigan to Minnesota. An Inc. magazine tech columnist and another passenger confirmed the offer, which started at $5,000 before increasing. A Delta spokesperson did not confirm the specific incident but stated that compensation is provided to ground staff for managing such situations to ensure flights depart on time.

feed.title: Rio Tinto markets low-emissions aluminum can
feed.summary: Rio Tinto, in partnership with Corona Canada and Ball Corp., has introduced "Canada's first specially-marked, low carbon beverage can." These 1.2 million cans, available in Ontario, utilize aluminum from Rio Tinto and incorporate Elysis technology, which produces aluminum without direct carbon dioxide emissions during smelting. This initiative, part of a collaboration with Anheuser-Busch InBev, combines renewable hydropower and recycled content, reducing carbon emissions by over 30 percent compared to conventional cans.

feed.title: Top 15 Affirm Competitors and Alternatives
feed.summary: This article identifies and details the top 15 competitors and alternatives to Affirm, a leading buy now, pay later (BNPL) service provider. It highlights various companies like Sezzle, PayPal, Klarna, Afterpay, and Zip, outlining their services, financial performance, and unique features. The piece also discusses alternatives that cater to specific needs, such as travel payments (Uplift) or those for consumers with poor credit (Perpay).

feed.title: Apple Pay: The ultimate guide
feed.summary: This comprehensive guide details Apple Pay, Apple's digital payment system designed to replace physical cards and cash with enhanced security and privacy. It covers setting up Apple Pay, utilizing the Wallet app, and explores related features such as Apple Card, Apple Cash, Apple Pay Express Transit, Apple Tap to Pay, and Apple Pay Later. The article highlights Apple Pay's evolution beyond contactless payments and its aim to simplify financial transactions.

feed.title: Deezer stock listing: Why did the Spotify rival crash on its first day of trading?
feed.summary: Deezer's stock crashed on its first day of trading in the French markets, despite initial high hopes for its public offering through a SPAC. The company's valuation significantly dropped from an anticipated €1.1 billion to €677 million. This poor performance is attributed to the intense competition from larger tech giants like Apple and Amazon, which have diverse revenue streams, and a general cooling of investor interest in tech stocks amidst rising inflation and recession fears in 2022.

feed.title: This Texas town is making itself into a semiconductor epicenter
feed.summary: Sherman, Texas, is transforming into a major semiconductor manufacturing hub, with significant investments from Texas Instruments and GlobalWafers totaling over $35 billion. This growth is driven by local leadership, existing infrastructure, and a pro-business tradition, positioning the city to play a critical role in the U.S. semiconductor supply chain. The expansion is expected to bring substantial economic benefits and population growth, though it also presents challenges related to rising housing costs and infrastructure demands.

feed.title: QQ Music introduces Dolby Atmos support
feed.summary: QQ Music, a leading Chinese music streaming service, has introduced Dolby Atmos support through a strategic partnership between Tencent Music Entertainment and Dolby. This integration brings spatial audio to the platform, making QQ Music the first domestic streaming service in China to offer Dolby Atmos, initially available to SVIP subscribers on compatible Android devices and featuring a growing catalog of international and Chinese artists. The collaboration aims to enhance the music listening experience for users in China.

feed.title: Gurus foresee 'virtual humans' enhancing many industries
feed.summary: Experts predict that virtual humans, fueled by AI advancements, will revolutionize various industries such as retail, finance, and education. Production costs and development times for digital avatars are expected to decrease significantly, leading to widespread adoption. Companies like Baidu are already investing heavily in this technology, with the market in China projected to reach 10.24 billion yuan ($1.5 billion) by 2026.

feed.title: GameStop Corp. Cl A stock falls Friday, underperforms market
feed.summary: Shares of GameStop Corp. Cl A (GME) fell 4.87% on Friday, closing at $128.54, an underperformance compared to the broader market and some competitors. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw slight declines, but GME's drop was more significant. The stock was $127.15 short of its 52-week high, and trading volume was below its 50-day average.

feed.title: What the Shopify stock split means for investors
feed.summary: This article discusses the implications of Shopify Inc.'s 10-to-1 stock split on June 29, 2022, following a significant drop in its share price. It explores the strategic benefits of such a split, including making shares more attractive to retail investors, improving liquidity, and potentially boosting the share price. The article also examines the initial impact on Shopify's stock performance and trading activity.

feed.title: The Unstoppable Rise of Formula 1
feed.summary: Formula 1 has experienced a remarkable resurgence, transitioning from declining viewership in 2016 to a global phenomenon by 2022. This growth is attributed to strategic marketing efforts by Liberty Media, particularly the Netflix series "Drive to Survive," and a new media rights deal with ESPN. The sport's increasing popularity is also reflected in increased viewership, significant revenue growth, and high-profile entertainment projects, making it a case study in effective content marketing for sports leagues.

feed.title: Apple Watch ‘Pro’ aims to be Garmin competitor – Here’s how much it could cost
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to launch an expensive 'Pro' model of the Apple Watch this year, alongside the standard Series 8 and a new Apple Watch SE. This "rugged" Pro model is designed to compete with high-end Garmin sports watches, targeting extreme sports athletes with features like a larger screen, bigger battery, S8 chip, body temperature sensor, and a durable non-aluminum metal case. The price is estimated to be around $900.

feed.title: ChromeLoader: New Stubborn Malware Campaign
feed.summary: This article details the ChromeLoader malware campaign, a browser hijacker and adware that surfaced in January 2022, spreading through malicious advertisements and primarily targeting users via compromised ISO/DMG files. The malware, also known as Choziosi Loader and ChromeBack, uses obfuscated browser extensions to steal search queries and display ads. The research identifies multiple evolving variants, including one targeting macOS, and emphasizes the attackers' rapid adaptation and sophisticated obfuscation techniques while highlighting protections offered by Palo Alto Networks.

feed.title: FlexShares Slashes Fees on U.S. Quality Large-Cap ETF
feed.summary: FlexShares has reduced the expense ratio for its US Quality Large Cap Index Fund (QLC B) from 0.32% to 0.25%, effective July 11, 2202. This move positions QLC among the least expensive offerings in its category, aiming to boost demand amidst market volatility. The fund tracks the Northern Trust Quality Large Cap Index, focusing on quality metrics for large-cap U.S. companies.

feed.title: All-New IHG One Rewards app powers loyalty, gives members more choice with Milestone Rewards
feed.summary: IHG Hotels & Resorts has launched an all-new IHG One Rewards app, significantly redesigning its mobile experience to enhance loyalty benefits and user control. The app introduces "Milestone Rewards" which allows members to choose personalized benefits like points or suite upgrades as they accumulate stays. It also features faster booking, exclusive offers, an account dashboard for tracking progress, and a digital concierge for improved customer service.

feed.title: Tesla alumni behind all-electric RV startup Lightship raise $23M
feed.summary: Lightship Energy, an all-electric RV startup founded by former Tesla employees Ben Parker and Toby Kraus, has raised $23 million in Series A funding. The company aims to revolutionize the RV industry by developing an electric travel trailer that eliminates propane tanks and generators, and, crucially, addresses the significant range reduction experienced by electric towing vehicles. Their design includes an underlying EV skateboard architecture and a battery pack capable of propelling the trailer, effectively making it a 300-mile trailer for a 300-mile truck.

feed.title: Unity to Buy IronSource in $4.4 Billion Deal to Boost Ad Tech
feed.summary: Unity Software Inc. is set to acquire app monetization company IronSource in an all-stock deal valued at approximately $4.4 billion to enhance its advertising technology. This move comes as Unity's ad tech has been affected by Apple's recent data-privacy changes, leading Unity to also lower its annual revenue forecast, which caused its shares to plunge 18%.

feed.title: General Mills, Fetch Rewards Roll Out Brand Loyalty Program
feed.summary: General Mills and Fetch Rewards have launched "Good Rewards," a new loyalty program offering exclusive deals and loyalty benefits on over 45 General Mills brands. Available through the Fetch app, this initiative aims to provide personalized, digital-first experiences and savings to consumers. Fetch Rewards allows shoppers to earn points by submitting receipts, which can then be redeemed for gift cards and merchandise.

feed.title: Prime Day 2022 was the biggest Prime Day event ever
feed.summary: Prime Day 2022 proved to be the largest Prime Day event in Amazon's history, with members purchasing over 300 million items worldwide and saving more than $1.7 billion. This year also marked the biggest Prime Day for Amazon's small and medium-sized selling partners, whose sales growth outpaced Amazon's retail business. Customers spent over $3 billion on small business items, highlighting a significant focus on supporting these businesses during the event.

feed.title: Chip Shortage Turns into Surplus
feed.summary: The global chip shortage, exacerbated by the coronavirus pandemic and high demand for electronics, has unexpectedly turned into a surplus due to high inflation, China's COVID restrictions, and the war in Ukraine, which reduced consumer spending. This shift has severely impacted semiconductor stocks, with companies like Micron Technology reducing production and manufacturers, especially in computing and smartphones, now facing an oversupply. The situation is worsened by hoarding practices, though some sectors like Apple suppliers and, for now, automobile chipmakers, are less affected.
feed.source: VOA - Voice of America English News
feed.source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News

feed.title: Taiwan accuses Chinese Apple supplier of stealing secrets, charges 14
feed.summary: Taiwanese prosecutors have accused Chinese Apple supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co Ltd of stealing commercial secrets and poaching staff from Taiwanese competitor Catcher Technology Co Ltd. The investigation, which lasted a year and a half, found that Luxshare specifically targeted Catcher's R&D team to gain orders from Apple. Consequently, 14 individuals have been charged with breach of trust and taking commercial secrets for overseas use.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. stock increased by 1.05% on Friday to $51.02, despite a strong day for the broader market indexes. The stock's rise ended a two-day losing streak, yet it still closed short of its 52-week high and underperformed key competitors like Apple, Alphabet, and AT&T. Trading volume for Verizon was lower than its 50-day average.

feed.title: Sonos Ray review: Finally a capable budget soundbar
feed.summary: The Sonos Ray is a new budget-friendly soundbar priced under $300, offering entry into the Sonos ecosystem. It provides excellent sound quality for its price, particularly with dialogue clarity, and supports Apple's AirPlay 2 and HomeKit. While compact and capable, its reliance on optical audio and limited remote support are notable drawbacks, especially in comparison to higher-end Sonos models like the Beam and Arc.

feed.title: +25년간 쉼없이 배당준 배당귀족주 ETF ; NOBL ETF 투자에 대해 공부해보기
feed.summary: This article discusses investing in the NOBL ETF, which focuses on Dividend Aristocrats—S&P 500 companies that have consistently increased dividends for at least 25 years. It explains the NOBL ETF's characteristics, including its investment strategy, current portfolio of 64 companies, and expense ratio, while noting its exclusion of major tech stocks like Apple and Microsoft. The author suggests it as a "peace-of-mind" investment during volatile market conditions.

feed.title: Daily deals July 17: $130 Apple TV 4K, $700 off 55-inch LG OLED Smart TV, $27 Netgear router, more
feed.summary: This article lists the best daily deals for July 17, including significant discounts on an Apple TV 4K, a 55-inch LG OLED Smart TV, and a Netgear router. It compiles offers from various online stores like Amazon and Walmart, advising readers to act quickly as deals can be short-lived. The piece also mentions ongoing deals from the previous day.

feed.title: Apple (AAPL) Plans to Slow Hiring and Spending for Some Teams Next Year
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) plans to decelerate its hiring and spending growth in certain divisions next year due to a potential economic downturn. This strategic decision is part of an effort to exercise caution during uncertain economic periods, though it does not apply universally across all teams. Despite these adjustments, Apple intends to maintain an ambitious product launch schedule for 2023, including the introduction of a mixed-reality headset.

feed.title: Lawsuit accuses Apple of antitrust violations over Apple Pay
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is facing a proposed class-action lawsuit filed by payment card issuers, accusing the company of antitrust violations related to its Apple Pay mobile wallet. The lawsuit alleges that Apple uses its market dominance in mobile devices to coerce consumers into using Apple Pay, imposing excess fees on banks and credit unions, unlike Android-based devices that allow users to choose other payment wallets. This legal action follows a previous warning from EU regulators regarding Apple's practices in mobile wallets.

feed.title: LinkedIn Still Number One Brand to be Faked in Phishing Attempts while Microsoft Surges up the Rankings to Number Two Spot in Q2 Report
feed.summary: Check Point Research's Q2 Brand Phishing Report reveals that LinkedIn remains the most imitated brand in phishing attempts, accounting for 45% of all such attacks. Microsoft has significantly risen to the second spot with 13%, surpassing DHL, while new brands like Adidas, Adobe, and HSBC have entered the top 10. The report highlights the dangers of these scams, with examples provided for LinkedIn, DHL, Outlook, and Amazon phishing attempts.

feed.title: Apple reaches $50 mln settlement over defective MacBook keyboards
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has agreed to a $50 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit concerning defective "butterfly" keyboards in its MacBook laptops. Customers claimed the keyboards were prone to failure and that Apple's repair program was inadequate. The settlement covers MacBooks, MacBook Airs, and MacBook Pros purchased between 2015 and 2019 in seven U.S. states, with individual payouts up to $395.

feed.title: Google faces $1 bln UK trial over app store pricing
feed.summary: Google is set to face a London trial over a £920 million ($1.1 billion) class action lawsuit alleging it overcharged 19.5 million customers for app store purchases. The lawsuit claims Google abused its dominant position by charging up to 30% commission on apps in its Play Store since October 2015, breaching European and British competition laws. This action is part of broader global efforts by regulators and consumer groups to curb alleged anti-competitive behavior by Big Tech companies like Google and Apple.

feed.title: Staple x Fossil Nate Sundial wristwatch
feed.summary: The article discusses how Fossil Group Inc. has been significantly impacted by the Apple Watch's release, leading to a substantial drop in its stock price. It highlights the launch of a unique limited-edition Fossil x Staple Nate Sundial watch, questioning whether this collaboration could mark a turnaround for the brand. Fossil's challenges are contrasted with the success of other traditional watch brands that have adapted to the market.

feed.title: JPMorgan adds former J&J chief Gorsky to board
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co has elected Alex Gorsky, the former chief executive officer of Johnson & Johnson, as a director of the bank. Gorsky, who is currently an executive chairman at J&J, previously led the development of J&J's COVID-19 vaccine and steered the company through significant crises. His appointment to JPMorgan's board is effective immediately.

feed.title: Who Will Build The Apple Car? Here Are The Possible Candidates
feed.summary: Apple's long-rumored "Project Titan" car initiative is inching closer to reality, but the company lacks automotive manufacturing experience. This article explores potential partners or strategies for building the Apple Car, including past talks with Hyundai-Kia, the LG/Magna joint venture, existing relationships with Volkswagen, and Foxconn's entry into EVs. It also considers the possibility of Apple manufacturing the car in-house, given its recruitment of top automotive talent and acquisition of self-driving technology.

feed.title: 3 Reasons Why Investing in the Nasdaq-100 Is Effective
feed.summary: This article highlights three core reasons why investing in the Nasdaq-100 is effective for long-term asset management. It explains how index investing, particularly in the Nasdaq-100, offers exposure to rapidly growing, innovative global companies with strong brand power and historically high growth rates. The article details various investment products available, such as mutual funds and ETFs, and discusses strategies like currency hedging and leverage.

feed.title: Semiconductor suppliers see oversupply of chips as smartphone demand falls
feed.summary: Semiconductor manufacturers are experiencing an oversupply of chips due to declining demand for smartphones and PCs, as indicated by executives from TSMC and Micron Technology. While TSMC anticipates a rebalancing of the supply chain in a few quarters, Micron expects a 10% year-over-year decline in PC and smartphone sales. Despite the current oversupply in the consumer sector, TSMC still foresees long-term demand exceeding supply, especially for automotive and data center customers, driven by the need for high-performance computing.

feed.title: Apple Argues It’s Now a Major Force in the Health-Care World
feed.summary: Apple has published a nearly 60-page report asserting its role as a pioneer in health technology and a significant force in the healthcare sector. The company highlights its existing health features, from sleep monitoring to atrial fibrillation detection, and plans to further innovate in science-based technology. This move indicates Apple's strategic focus on health as a future growth driver.

feed.title: Netflix Lost Nearly 1 Million Subscribers Last Quarter. Here’s What That Means for Its Future
feed.summary: Netflix reported a loss of nearly 1 million subscribers in the second quarter of 2022, compounding its 200,000 subscriber loss from the previous quarter. Despite this, the total loss was less than projected, leading to a temporary stock surge, though shares remain significantly down for the year. In response, Netflix is pursuing efforts like an ad-supported tier and focusing on high-quality original content to drive future growth and revenue.

feed.title: FaZe Clan Has Gone Public
feed.summary: Gaming and lifestyle media platform FaZe Clan has officially gone public, completing a business combination with B. Riley Principal 150 Merger Corp. Shares began trading under "FAZE" and "FAZEW" at a $725 million valuation. With this move, FaZe Clan aims to access capital for growth and allow fans to own part of its future, leveraging its influence among Gen Z.

feed.title: T-Mobile And Apple To Sell Small-business Connectivity Plan
feed.summary: T-Mobile US Inc. announced a partnership with Apple Inc. to offer a new mobile phone plan targeting small-business owners. This plan includes subsidized iPhone 13s, T-Mobile wireless service, and Apple's Business Essentials service, with pricing as low as $50 per month for businesses with six or more lines. The initiative aims to streamline device management and connectivity for businesses with limited IT resources.

feed.title: 7 Reasons Why People Are Moving to Texas
feed.summary: Texas has become a popular relocation destination, drawing nearly 4 million new residents between 2010 and 2020. This article explores the key factors attracting people to the Lone Star State, including a lower cost of living, more affordable housing, no state income tax, and a thriving job market. Additionally, Texas boasts great schools, a warm climate, and rich cultural diversity, making it an appealing option for many.

feed.title: TSLQ Stock Price Today | AXS TSLA Bear Daily ETF
feed.summary: This page provides detailed information on the AXS TSLA Bear Daily ETF (TSLQ), including its current stock price, historical data, and key statistics. It also offers technical analysis, holdings, and related news, presenting TSLQ's performance as of May 14, 2026. The ETF is managed by AXS Investments LLC and employs a short strategy using derivatives.

feed.title: iRhythm Technologies receives another FDA 510(k) for AFib monitoring system
feed.summary: iRhythm Technologies has received another FDA 510(k) clearance for its ZEUS System, developed in partnership with Verily, which monitors atrial fibrillation. The latest clearance is for an algorithm that characterizes the amount of AFib a patient experiences over time using PPG-based bio-signals. The ZEUS System is not yet commercially available but is expected to have a limited market release in 2023.

feed.title: GM trails far behind Tesla in EV sales — CEO Mary Barra bet the company that will change
feed.summary: GM CEO Mary Barra is betting the company's future on electric vehicles, despite currently trailing far behind Tesla in sales. GM plans to invest $30 billion in EVs by 2025, launch 30 electric models globally, and believes its Ultium platform will enable it to surpass Tesla by mid-decade or 2029. Barra's legacy is tied to transforming GM into an electric leader, building on past efforts to make the company leaner and more agile.

feed.title: SCHB ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:SCHB
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the Schwab U.S. Broad Market ETF (SCHB), detailing its dividends, returns, and key characteristics. It covers essential information such as assets under management, expense ratio, inception date, and the index it tracks. The article also breaks down the fund's holdings by sector and region, and answers frequently asked questions about SCHB's investments, dividends, and performance.

feed.title: SCHX ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:SCHX
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the Schwab U.S. Large-Cap ETF (SCHX), covering its dividends, returns, and key characteristics. It details the fund's asset allocation, top holdings, expense ratio, and replication strategy. The analysis also includes recent performance metrics, fund flows, and answers frequently asked questions about SCHX.

feed.title: Tuya Announces Matter-compatible Solutions in Close Collaboration with Amazon Alexa
feed.summary: Tuya Smart announced its collaboration with Amazon Alexa to implement Frustration-Free Setup (FFS) based on the Matter connectivity protocol, aiming to enhance the IoT device setup experience and allow direct Wi-Fi connections to Amazon Echo devices. Tuya, an initial FFS partner for Matter, is participating in interoperability tests with Amazon, Google, and Apple and plans to launch Matter-compatible devices by the end of 2022. The company is committed to promoting universal open standards in IoT and supporting its ecosystem partners in adopting Matter, enabling a wider variety of smart home solutions for consumers.

feed.title: iRhythm, Alphabet unit Verily get FDA clearances for Zio Watch and software system
feed.summary: iRhythm Technologies and Alphabet's Verily have received FDA 510(k) clearance for their new AI-powered Zio Watch and Zio ECG Utilization Software System (Zeus). This system is designed for continuous monitoring of cardiac arrhythmia, particularly atrial fibrillation, and aims to assist clinicians in diagnosis by characterizing the amount of AFib over time. While not yet commercially available, the collaboration aims to compete with existing wearables like the Apple Watch in the cardiac monitoring market.

feed.title: Workers For Lululemon DC Store File To Form Union
feed.summary: Employees at a Lululemon store in Washington, D.C., have filed a petition to unionize, forming a group called the "Association of Concerted Educators." This move is part of a growing trend of unionization efforts at major U.S. retail companies, with employees seeking demands such as increased pay transparency and equitable pay structures. Lululemon has acknowledged the petition, stating its respect for the process and commitment to ongoing dialogue with its teams.

feed.title: Wafers and layers: Fab advancements at China’s YMTC
feed.summary: China's Yangtze Memory Technology Corp (YMTC) is rapidly advancing its NAND technology, reportedly developing 196 and 232-layer NAND products and building a second fabrication plant to significantly increase production capacity. The company aims to expand its market share beyond 5 percent and is even in discussions with Apple. These developments underscore China's push for technological self-sufficiency in the memory sector.

feed.title: Gainesville SWAT team accused of subjecting people to unconstitutional search and seizure
feed.summary: Three individuals have filed a federal lawsuit against the City of Gainesville, Gainesville Police, and members of its SWAT team, alleging unconstitutional search and seizure during a 2021 raid. The lawsuit claims that despite not being named on the search warrant or accused of a crime, they were held at gunpoint, zip-tied, detained for hours, and had personal property seized, which was only returned months later after legal action. The plaintiffs argue the search warrant was based on flawed information and that the GPD's policy makes it too easy to deploy the SWAT team for search warrants, leading to civil rights violations.

feed.title: Top stock reports to watch this week (25.07.2022)
feed.summary: The article highlights the key earnings reports scheduled for the week of July 25, 2022, focusing on major US tech companies like Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Amazon, and Apple. It notes that the Wall Street earnings season is in full swing, with several Dow Jones members also reporting their financial results. This week is expected to be significant for traders due to the high-profile companies announcing their earnings.

feed.title: Apple, Koss Reach Deal in AirPods Patent Case on Eve of Trial
feed.summary: Apple Inc. and headphone maker Koss Corp. have reached a settlement in their patent-infringement dispute over Apple's AirPods. The agreement, whose terms were not disclosed, led to the cancellation of a trial that was scheduled to begin in a Texas federal court. Koss had sued Apple, alleging infringement of five technology patents.

feed.title: Metaverse ETF Full of Long-Term Potential
feed.summary: Despite recent underperformance by growth stocks, the metaverse investment thesis is still in its early stages, presenting a long-term opportunity. The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET A-) is highlighted as a potentially well-timed investment, given forecasts predicting the metaverse market could grow from $40-60 billion in 2021 to $8-13 trillion by 2030. FMET offers broad exposure to companies like Alphabet, Apple, Meta Platforms, and Tencent, which are integral to the metaverse's growth across various technologies.

feed.title: Metaverse ETF Full of Long-Term Potential
feed.summary: The article discusses the long-term potential of the metaverse as an investment opportunity, particularly through the Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET). Despite a challenging year for growth stocks, the metaverse market is projected to grow significantly, reaching trillions by 2030, according to major investment banks. FMET offers a diversified approach to capitalize on this growth, including holdings in major tech companies like Alphabet, Apple, Meta Platforms, and Tencent.

feed.title: Snap Plunges, Drags Down Social Media ETF
feed.summary: Snap Inc. shares significantly dropped after reporting weak quarterly results due to slowing advertisement revenue, impacting the entire social media sector and the Global X Social Media ETF (SOCL). This downturn follows increased competition for ad dollars, particularly from TikTok, leading analysts to lower their recommendations and price targets for Snap. The company's struggles may reflect broader challenges within the tech and social media industries, with Snap planning reduced hiring, mirroring other major tech companies.

feed.title: Intellectual property enforcement nets Koss Corp. up to $14 million
feed.summary: Milwaukee-based Koss Corp. is set to generate $10 million to $14 million in net proceeds during Q4 from its intellectual property enforcement program, significantly surpassing its typical quarterly revenue. This windfall comes after settling a patent infringement lawsuit with Apple Inc., part of ongoing litigation against several major audio companies over wireless audio technology. Other lawsuits filed in 2020 against competitors like Bose and Skullcandy are still pending.

feed.title: Apple's earnings will give a peek into the mindset of the high-end consumer
feed.summary: Apple is set to report its third-quarter earnings, which analysts and investors will closely scrutinize for insights into the impact of macroeconomic trends like inflation and rising interest rates on high-end consumer spending. While Apple's sales have historically been robust due to its affluent customer base, any indication of slowing demand for iPhones and Macs could signal broader economic concerns. The company's performance will also reflect its ability to manage supply chain issues and COVID-19 lockdowns in China, as well as the growth trajectory of its services business.

feed.title: Tencent tops Apple, Sony to take gaming market crown
feed.summary: Tencent Holdings has overtaken industry giants like Sony and Apple to become the leading company in the gaming market, primarily through over 180 strategic, smaller investments worldwide. Despite regulatory challenges in China and increased competition from large acquisitions by Microsoft and Sony, Tencent is intensifying its focus on overseas markets, planning a global release for its top game "Honor of Kings," as its value-added services segment remains highly profitable. The Chinese tech giant's diverse global portfolio has allowed it to achieve significant revenue figures even as it navigates a changing domestic and international landscape.

feed.title: SK Hynix Profit Jumps 56% on Resilient Chip Demand, Weak Won
feed.summary: SK Hynix reported a 56% surge in second-quarter profit due to strong chip demand and a weak Korean won. However, the company, like other tech giants, warned of an impending slowdown in demand for its key products like PCs, smartphones, and servers, leading to a cautious outlook and plans to adjust 2023 capital expenditure.

feed.title: Tiny Koss Scores Big Win over Giant Apple in Patent Dispute Settlement
feed.summary: Koss Corporation has settled its patent infringement dispute with Apple Inc., securing an estimated $10-$14 million in licensing fees. This significant win for the smaller audio company, which had filed suit in 2020 alleging infringement of its wireless audio patents, underscores the growing trend of smaller innovators successfully challenging tech giants. The settlement not only provides a financial windfall for Koss but also strengthens the legitimacy of its patent portfolio for future legal actions.

feed.title: Why Spotify's 'Car Thing' was destined for the hardware graveyard
feed.summary: Spotify has discontinued its "Car Thing" player, a device designed to stream music and podcasts from a user's phone in the car. The company cited an inability to achieve an "attractive economical profile" due to low sales, high component costs, and chip shortages. This move highlights the difficulties software companies face when venturing into hardware, especially when competing with established ecosystems like Apple CarPlay and Android Auto.

feed.title: Apple Outperforms in Q3 2022 Despite a Rocky Road
feed.summary: Apple announced its Q3 2022 financial results, showing a 2% increase in revenue to $83.0 billion but a 10% decrease in profits year-over-year. Despite a challenging global economic climate, the iPhone and Services sectors saw growth, while iPad, Mac, and Wearables experienced declines largely due to supply constraints. Apple still managed to beat analyst expectations, and CEO Tim Cook expressed optimism about the company's resilience.

feed.title: Apple forecasts faster sales growth, strong iPhone demand despite glum economy
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL.O) reported stronger-than-expected fiscal third-quarter results, driven by robust iPhone demand, despite a global economic slowdown and supply chain constraints. The company's shares rose 3.5% after hours, as it forecasted faster sales growth for the current quarter and noted easing parts shortages. While macroeconomic indicators are turning negative, Apple's Chief Financial Officer Luca Maestri stated that iPhone demand remains strong, showcasing the resilience of its affluent customer base.

feed.title: Apple Narrowly Tops Estimates as iPhone Fares Better Than Feared
feed.summary: Apple Inc. narrowly beat analyst estimates for its fiscal third-quarter revenue and profit, driven by better-than-expected iPhone sales. Despite a "cocktail of headwinds," CEO Tim Cook anticipates sales will rebound in the coming months, offering reassurance to investors worried about an economic slowdown. This performance gives Apple time to prepare for new product launches.

feed.title: Apple Supplier TSMC's Arizona Plant Nears Completion, Expected to Mass Produce 5nm Chips
feed.summary: TSMC, a key Apple chip supplier, recently held a topping-out ceremony for its new $12 billion fabrication plant in Phoenix, Arizona. This factory is set to mass-produce 5nm chips starting in the first quarter of 2024, which will be the first time Apple's custom silicon chips of this size could be produced in the U.S. Despite Apple's rumored shift to 3nm processes, the iPhone 14 Pro's A16 chip is expected to use 5nm technology, ensuring continued demand for TSMC's Arizona output.

feed.title: Walmart Hawks Refurbished Goods to Inflation-Squeezed Shoppers
feed.summary: Walmart is launching a "Walmart Restored" program to sell refurbished goods from brands like Apple, Samsung, and KitchenAid, both online and in some stores this fall. This initiative aims to attract cost-conscious shoppers amidst high inflation by offering inspected, tested, and cleaned products with 90-day free returns. The move comes after Walmart cut its profit forecast, noting that rising food and fuel prices are causing customers to reduce spending on general merchandise.

feed.title: Touchstone US Large Cap Focused ETF Holdings
feed.summary: The Touchstone US Large Cap Focused ETF (LCF) was established on July 29, 2022, concentrating its investments in large-cap equities. The ETF currently manages $64.97 million in assets across 47 holdings, focusing on US-listed large-cap stocks with sustainable competitive advantages or high barriers to entry. Top holdings include Alphabet Inc., Microsoft Corp., NVIDIA Corp., and Apple Inc.

feed.title: Apple’s Hot iPhone Quarter Masks a Behind-the-Scenes Slowdown
feed.summary: Apple experienced a strong quarter for iPhone sales, however, the company is facing an unapparent slowdown. Analyst Mark Gurman also notes Apple's acquisition of a top Lamborghini executive and Meta's interest in an eye-tracking startup. This article follows Gurman's previous assessment of Apple's vulnerability during an economic downturn.

feed.title: Webull Launches Expansion into Singapore
feed.summary: Webull Corporation has officially launched its expansion into Singapore and the Asia-Pacific region. Through Webull Securities (Singapore) Pte. Ltd., residents can now trade U.S. and Hong Kong-listed equities, ETFs, options, and fractional shares with low commissions. This move follows Webull's success in the U.S. and Hong Kong, aiming to capture the growing mobile-first brokerage market in Asia-Pacific.

feed.title: Pontifications: Two books examine GE’s fall from grace
feed.summary: This article reviews two books about General Electric and its former CEO Jack Welch: "Lights Out: Pride, Delusion, and the Fall of General Electric" and "The Man Who Broke Capitalism." The author, Scott Hamilton, suggests that "Lights Out" provides a more detailed corporate history, while "The Man Who Broke Capitalism" offers a personal biography of Welch and discusses his influence on leaders at other companies like Boeing. The review highlights how Welch's management style and GE's culture, focused on short-term shareholder value and cost-cutting, adversely affected other companies, including Boeing, leading to a "damning picture" of negative impact on American industry.

feed.title: Apple buys HP’s 67-acre San Diego campus as it expands footprint and works on custom modem
feed.summary: Apple has purchased HP's former 67-acre campus in San Diego for $445 million, marking a significant investment in its engineering efforts in Southern California. The acquisition is intended to support Apple's expanding work on an in-house modem and other wireless technologies, with plans to increase local job creation from 1,200 to 5,000 by 2026. This move reinforces Apple's commitment to bringing more hardware components in-house, despite potential delays in its own modem deployment.

feed.title: Music Stocks List 2026 - Best Publicly Traded Companies
feed.summary: This article identifies top publicly traded music companies for 2026, including Spotify, Apple, Sirius XM, Google, Tencent Music Entertainment, and Warner Music Group. It discusses their market positions, growth opportunities, and business models, highlighting shifts towards streaming, podcasts, and diverse media portfolios within the evolving music industry. The author emphasizes that investing in the music industry offers various choices, from streaming giants to production houses, with significant future growth potential.

feed.title: Alibaba Cloud and XPeng Motors build autonomous vehicle cloud data center
feed.summary: Alibaba Cloud and XPeng Motors have partnered to establish an "intelligent computing center for automated driving" in Ulanqab, Inner Mongolia, aiming to accelerate the development and testing of autonomous vehicle models. The facility boasts a claimed computing power of 600 petaflops and uses efficient air-cooling technology. This initiative is part of China's "Eastern Data Western Calculation" strategy, leveraging the region's favorable conditions for data centers.

feed.title: YC and a16z back virtual reality basketball app Gym Class
feed.summary: Virtual reality basketball app Gym Class has secured an $8 million seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz, even as the broader VR startup ecosystem faces challenges. The app, which is a VR pure-play and currently has nearly 1 million downloads on Meta’s App Lab, is viewed as a social hub for basketball enthusiasts. Its upcoming launch on the official Quest Store is a significant step, highlighting the critical role Meta's platform plays for VR developers.

feed.title: Microstrategy Outperforms Every Asset Class and Big Tech Stock Since Adopting Bitcoin Strategy, Says CEO
feed.summary: Microstrategy CEO Michael Saylor highlights that the company's bitcoin strategy has led to significant outperformance against all asset classes and major tech stocks since its adoption. Saylor attributes this success to bitcoin's digital property characteristics and its appeal as a safe haven asset. The company's unique approach involved acquiring substantial bitcoin holdings and integrating it into their corporate strategy.

feed.title: Pelosi’s Taiwan trip puts the world's biggest chipmaker back in the spotlight of U.S.-China rivalry
feed.summary: Nancy Pelosi's visit to Taiwan brought renewed attention to the island's critical role in the global chip supply chain, specifically highlighting Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), a key manufacturer for tech giants like Apple and Nvidia. The visit underscored the strategic importance of semiconductors to U.S. national security and its rivalry with China. TSMC's dominance in the foundry market and the ongoing U.S. efforts to boost domestic chip manufacturing and secure supply chains were central themes, with concerns about China's potential actions towards Taiwan posing a significant risk to the global chip market.

feed.title: Apple's HR department comes under fire
feed.summary: A Financial Times report reveals that Apple's HR department is facing accusations from over a dozen current and former female employees of mishandling misconduct allegations and retaliating against those who complain. Many claim that voicing concerns led to them being labeled "bad team members" and ultimately resulted in their departure or a severance offer in exchange for releasing Apple from liability. This comes amidst ongoing employee activism, including the #AppleToo movement, which has seen hundreds of accounts of misconduct reported by current and former Apple staff.

feed.title: Nio sets up mobile technology company, first phone coming next year - ArenaEV
feed.summary: Nio has officially established "Nio Mobile Technologies Co Ltd" with a registered capital of $100 million, signaling its entry into the smartphone market. The company aims to develop and sell electronic products, mobile devices, and wearable devices, with a self-developed phone expected next year. Nio's CEO confirmed plans for an annual phone release, designed to integrate seamlessly with Nio vehicles and offered at a competitive price compared to Apple products.

feed.title: Jamf Holding Corp. (JAMF) Q2 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Jamf Holding Corp. reported strong Q2 2022 earnings, exceeding expectations with 40% ARR growth and 34% revenue growth. The company highlighted significant expansion in commercial and education markets, driven by product diversification and a growing demand for Apple device management and security solutions. Key wins included Red Bull consolidating Apple devices with Jamf Pro and a large education program in Taiwan adopting Jamf for 80% of its schools, despite an uncertain macroeconomic environment and hardware supply issues.

feed.title: The African “Silicon Valley” Underway
feed.summary: South Africa, particularly Cape Town and the Western Cape, is emerging as a strong contender for "the next Silicon Valley" due to its robust tech ecosystem, high foreign direct investment, and a significant number of tech startups and venture capital firms. The region boasts a high concentration of developer talent, world-class universities, and an environment conducive to doing business, mirroring key factors that contributed to the success and sustained innovation of the original Silicon Valley. These attributes, combined with a focus on cultural diversity and strong educational institutions, position South Africa as an innovation hub on the African continent.

feed.title: Apple M1 Macs: What you need to know about buying an Apple silicon Mac
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to Apple's M1, M1 Pro, M1 Max, and M1 Ultra chips, detailing their specifications, the Mac models they power, and their impact on software compatibility and accessory usage. It also addresses common questions about RAM limitations, Intel Mac support, and the future of Apple silicon. The piece emphasizes the performance and battery life benefits of M1 Macs while advising potential buyers on compatibility checks and future market trends.

feed.title: Large Indiana employers Eli Lilly and Cummins speak out about the state's new restrictive abortion law
feed.summary: Large Indiana employers Eli Lilly and Cummins have voiced strong opposition to the state's newly passed restrictive abortion law. Eli Lilly stated the law would hinder its ability to attract diverse talent and force it to plan for employment growth outside Indiana, while Cummins expressed concerns about its impact on talent attraction and retention. Both companies emphasized that the law conflicts with ensuring women's full participation in the workforce and the need for inclusive environments.

feed.title: Apple’s iPad Software Delay Keeps Focus on Its Main Attraction: iPhone 14
feed.summary: Apple has postponed the release of iPadOS 16 until October, a move that is considered a proper decision but could lead to new challenges for users and developers. This delay shifts the spotlight to the upcoming iPhone 14, as Apple concurrently prepares for its September keynote event. The article also notes potential unionization efforts in more Apple stores.

feed.title: JPMorgan Active Growth ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:JGRO) $JGRO
feed.summary: The JPMorgan Active Growth ETF (JGRO) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund that invests in U.S. large-cap companies with strong earnings growth potential. Launched on August 8, 2022, the ETF has $8.27 billion in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.44%. As of March 13, 2026, JGRO was trading at $85.77, a 7.6% decrease from its Jan 1, 2026 price of $92.80, and its top holdings include technology giants like NVIDIA, Apple, and Alphabet.

feed.title: Direxion Launches Apple & Tesla Single-Stock ETFs
feed.summary: Direxion has launched the first four of its single-stock leveraged and inverse ETFs, offering sophisticated traders magnified or inverse daily exposure to Apple and Tesla stock. These new ETFs are designed for short-term tactical trading in volatile markets and are not intended as buy-and-hold investments due to their high-risk nature and lack of diversification. They aim to provide active traders with tools to respond to company events and market sentiment.

feed.title: PhonePe Enables Seamless Purchase of App Store Codes
feed.summary: PhonePe, India's leading digital payments platform, has enabled the purchase of Apple's App Store Codes through its app. Users can buy these codes, ranging from Rs 100 to Rs 10,000, to pay for digital content like apps, games, music, and more on the App Store without needing a debit/credit card. This service was made possible through a partnership with epay, a segment of Euronet Worldwide, Inc., Apple's distributor for App Store Codes in India.

feed.title: AAPU Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:AAPU
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Direxion Daily AAPL Bull 2X ETF (AAPU), a short-term tactical tool designed to provide 2x the daily price return of Apple stock. It covers key statistics like Assets Under Management, dividend yield, and expense ratio, along with information on its issuer, inception date, and investment strategy. The article also includes performance data, technical analysis, and answers to frequently asked questions about the ETF.

feed.title: AAPD Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:AAPD
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of the Direxion Daily AAPL Bear 1X ETF (AAPD), detailing its current price, performance metrics, and key statistics such as Assets Under Management (AUM) and expense ratio. It also explains that AAPD is a short-term tactical tool designed to deliver -1x the daily price return of Apple stock, highlighting its inverse exposure and the need for frequent rebalancing for holders. The article includes news, technical analysis summaries, and answers to frequently asked questions about the ETF.

feed.title: How Microsoft secures its spot among the world's most valuable brands
feed.summary: Microsoft has become one of the world's most valuable brands, with its brand value reaching $661 billion in 2022, securing the number four spot in Kantar BrandZ Most Valuable Global Brands. The company's success is attributed to its meaningful, different, and salient offerings, including superior cloud, software, and hardware products, as well as its commitment to sustainability. Under CEO Satya Nadella, Microsoft has focused on specific market segments, leading to a tenfold increase in brand value and solidifying its position as the world's most valuable business services brand, further boosted by the continued digital transformation and remote work trends.

feed.title: 4 Index Funds to Buy and Hold Forever
feed.summary: This article recommends four index funds to consider buying and holding forever: Fidelity Nasdaq Composite Index ETF (ONEQ), Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Financials ETF (RYF), SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF Trust (DIA), and SPDR S&P Semiconductor ETF (XSD). The author suggests that the 2022 market selloff provides an opportune moment to invest in these diversified options, highlighting their low costs, reduced capital gains liability, and ability to build a robust portfolio. Each fund is detailed with its specific focus, holdings, performance metrics, and expense ratios.

feed.title: Invisalign Photo Uploader by Align Technology, Inc.
feed.summary: The Invisalign Photo Uploader application, developed by Align Technology, Inc., allows Invisalign Providers and their staff to easily create patient profiles, take high-quality photos, and submit them to the Invisalign Doctor Site. The app offers features like guided photo-taking, auto-cropping, quality analysis, and direct upload, alongside an option to export photos for doctor records. It is intended exclusively for Invisalign providers.

feed.title: Desktop CPU Sales Lowest in 30 Years, AMD Gains Market Share Anyway (Updated)
feed.summary: Desktop CPU shipments hit a nearly 30-year low in Q2 2022, marking the largest year-over-year decline in Mercury Research's records. Despite the overall market slump, AMD significantly increased its market share and revenue in desktop, mobile, and server segments, setting new records for x86 processor share and mobile unit share. Intel, in contrast, reported a rare loss and delayed key products, indicating a challenging period for the long-standing leader.

feed.title: Download and Play Spellslingers Now!
feed.summary: Wizards of the Coast has soft-launched Spellslingers, a new digital collectible card game set in the Magic: The Gathering multiverse. The game features fast-paced, hero-centric combat, allowing players to choose from sixteen unique Spellslinger characters, each with their own playstyle and starter deck. Spellslingers is available for download on Android, iOS, and PC, inviting players to customize their decks, form teams, and trade materials.

feed.title: This weekend, I watched a hacker jailbreak a John Deere tractor live on stage
feed.summary: The author describes watching a hacker named Sickcodes jailbreak a John Deere tractor's control unit at Defcon 30. This event highlighted John Deere's monopolistic practices regarding tractor repair and its flawed information security, despite the company's claims that locking down tractors protects farmers and copyright. Sickcodes's hack revealed the company's use of outdated software and poor security protocols, demonstrating that their "security through obscurity" approach makes them vulnerable, not secure.

feed.title: Intel hands over nearly 5,000 patents in deal with IP management outfit
feed.summary: Intel has transferred a portfolio of nearly 5,000 patents to Tahoe Research Limited, a new company within IPValue Management Group, which will license them to third parties. This deal aims to maximize revenue from Intel's older, non-core patents by leveraging IPValue's expertise in intellectual property management and licensing. This move follows Intel's past sales of its smartphone modem business to Apple and NAND flash business to SK hynix, indicating a strategy to streamline its IP portfolio.

feed.title: Apple Sets Return-to-Office Deadline of Sept. 5 After Covid Delays
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has set a September 5 deadline for corporate employees to return to the office for at least three days a week. This marks another attempt to implement its hybrid work model after previous delays due to COVID-19 surges. Employees will be required to work in-person on Tuesdays, Thursdays, and a third day that individual teams will decide, a change from the initial Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday plan.

feed.title: Buffett's Berkshire boosts Ally, Activision holdings; sheds Verizon
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway significantly increased its stakes in Ally Financial and Activision Blizzard while completely exiting its investment in Verizon Communications by June 30. The conglomerate, run by Warren Buffett, also slowed its overall stock buying in the second quarter, making fewer new purchases and sales compared to the first quarter. Berkshire's portfolio continues to be dominated by Apple, with substantial investments in oil companies Chevron and Occidental Petroleum.

feed.title: Apple Lays Off Recruiters as Part of Its Slowdown in Hiring
feed.summary: Apple Inc. laid off approximately 100 contract-based recruiters, a move reflecting the tech giant's effort to slow hiring and reduce spending. This action, although rare for Apple, signals a broader slowdown within the company. The affected recruiters were responsible for bringing in new employees, underscoring a shift in Apple's staffing strategy.

feed.title: Google Strikes Back at Sonos; SmartSpeaker War Gets Ugly
feed.summary: Google has sued Sonos for patent infringement, escalating an ongoing battle over smart home audio technology. This comes after Sonos previously won a ruling against Google regarding similar claims. The conflict highlights broader issues of antitrust concerns and data privacy in the smart speaker market, with Sonos launching its own privacy-focused voice assistant.

feed.title: The Blue Light blues
feed.summary: The article discusses the pervasive issue of blue light exposure from screens and its detrimental effects on human health, including disrupted sleep, eyestrain, headaches, and accelerated skin aging. It explains what blue light is, how it impacts circadian rhythms, and the potential benefits of blue light-blocking glasses. The piece also highlights a study on fruit flies linking blue light to neurological damage and offers advice on minimizing exposure.

feed.title: Qualcomm planning return to server market with new chip - Bloomberg News
feed.summary: Qualcomm Inc. is reportedly planning a return to the server market with a new chip, aiming to reduce its dependence on smartphones. The company is seeking customers for a product based on its Nuvia Inc. acquisition, with Amazon Web Services (AWS) agreeing to evaluate the offerings. This move comes as Qualcomm faces a slowdown in smartphone demand, impacting its core handset chip business.

feed.title: Charles Schwab and Pinnacle Lose Options Strategy Arbitration Case
feed.summary: Charles Schwab and Pinnacle Associates, an RIA firm, have been ordered to pay a former customer approximately $800,000. This award stems from an arbitration case where the customer alleged misrepresentation regarding an options strategy, leading to unexpected sales of her Apple shares. The case highlights risks associated with certain investment strategies and the importance of clear communication between advisors and clients.

feed.title: Paramount renews U.S. rights to Champions League valued at $1.5 billion
feed.summary: Paramount Global has renewed its U.S. media rights for the UEFA Champions League in a six-year deal valued at over $1.5 billion, more than double its previous contract. This agreement, running from 2024 to 2030, reflects the growing popularity of football in the U.S. and further emphasizes the rising cost of sports broadcasting rights. Paramount will pay approximately $250 million annually to air the matches on its CBS network and Paramount+ streaming service.

feed.title: Why the ESG/Tech Combination Is Important
feed.summary: Broad market environmental, social, and governance (ESG) exchange-traded funds (ETFs) frequently have significant allocations to technology stocks. For example, the Invesco ESG Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQMG) has a technology weight of almost 62%. While some critics view this as over-reliance on tech, experts argue that technology companies often embody sustainable development principles, making them suitable for ESG funds despite facing scrutiny on issues like data privacy and energy consumption.

feed.title: Apple expands Self Service Repair to Mac notebooks
feed.summary: Apple has expanded its Self Service Repair program to include MacBook Air and MacBook Pro notebooks with M1 family chips, making genuine parts, tools, and manuals available to US customers. This initiative allows individuals experienced with electronic device repair to fix their own MacBooks, with options to rent tool kits and receive credit for returning replaced parts. The program, which launched earlier for iPhone, is part of Apple's broader effort to increase access to repairs, including supporting a growing network of authorized service providers and independent repair shops.

feed.title: Ex-Apple engineer admits stealing automotive trade secrets
feed.summary: A former Apple engineer named Xiaolang Zhang pleaded guilty to stealing confidential automotive trade secrets from Apple. Zhang was indicted in 2018 for obtaining a 25-page document detailing a circuit board for Apple's autonomous vehicle project, shortly after announcing his departure to work for Chinese EV maker Xpeng. Xpeng has denied involvement in the case, while also being a competitor in the autonomous driving space.

feed.title: SanDisk Professional G-Drive range overhauled with 10Gbit USB 3.2 Type-C
feed.summary: SanDisk Professional has updated its G-Drive range with a new hard disk-based external drive featuring a 10Gbit USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C interface and a refreshed design. The stackable, anodized aluminum enclosure includes an Ultrastar enterprise-class hard drive for reliability, offering up to 22TB of storage. Preformatted for Mac and compatible with Windows after reformatting, the drives boast speeds up to 250MB/s and are available for preorder starting at $209.99 for a 4TB model.

feed.title: Tinder-owner Match Group files antitrust case against Apple with India’s competition regulator
feed.summary: Match Group, owner of Tinder, has filed an antitrust case against Apple with India's competition regulator, accusing the tech giant of monopolistic conduct by forcing developers to use its proprietary in-app purchase system and charging excessive commissions. This action adds to existing challenges against Apple in India and globally regarding its App Store policies. Match argues Apple's practices restrict innovation and unfairly differentiate between app types, especially concerning payment methods favored in India.

feed.title: CVNets: High Performance Library for Computer Vision
feed.summary: Apple Machine Learning Research introduces CVNets, an open-source library designed for high-performance training of deep neural networks in visual recognition. It supports various tasks like classification, detection, and segmentation, offering tools for image and video understanding, including data loading and transformations. The library also features implementations of standard networks with competitive or superior performance, aiming to streamline computer vision research and development.

feed.title: AMD Set To Become TSMC’s Second Largest 5nm Customer Courtesy Of Ryzen 7000
feed.summary: AMD is projected to become TSMC's second-largest customer for 5nm technology, primarily driven by its upcoming Ryzen 7000 series processors. These new CPUs, including desktop, notebook, and datacenter variants, will extensively utilize TSMC's 5nm process node. This increased demand will solidify AMD's position as a major client for the Taiwanese chip manufacturer, second only to Apple.

feed.title: Lucid Appoints Derrick Carty as Vice President of Platform Software Engineering
feed.summary: Lucid Group, Inc. announced the appointment of Derrick Carty as Vice President of Platform Software Engineering, reporting to Michael Bell, Senior Vice President of Digital. In this newly created role, Mr. Carty will lead software engineering for the Lucid Air, encompassing ADAS, Audio, CoreOS/Android Framework, Embedded Software, Controls Platform, AUTOSAR, and Systems Architecture. Carty brings 23 years of experience from Apple, Inc. and will focus on delivering new Lucid Air features via over-the-air software updates.

feed.title: Smartwatch Market Size Will Achieve USD 156.3 Billion by 2030 growing at 20.1% CAGR - Exclusive Report by Acumen Research and Consulting
feed.summary: The global smartwatch market is projected to reach USD 156.3 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 20.1% from 2022 to 2030, according to a report by Acumen Research and Consulting. Key drivers include rising demand for wearable devices with advanced features, increasing consumer spending on healthcare, and the impact of COVID-19 which highlighted the health monitoring capabilities of smartwatches. North America is expected to hold the largest market share, while Asia-Pacific is anticipated to show the fastest growth.

feed.title: Apple’s Chief Privacy Officer Set to Leave Company for Law Firm
feed.summary: Apple Inc.'s Chief Privacy Officer, Jane Horvath, is set to leave the company to join the law firm Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP. Horvath, who started at Apple in 2011, held a significant role, particularly as privacy became a key selling point for the iPhone maker. Her departure was recently communicated internally to Apple's legal department.

feed.title: Google Says Trump’s Truth Social Must Scrub Violent Content to Join Play Store (Published 2022)
feed.summary: Google stated it would not distribute Truth Social on its Play Store until the platform removed content inciting violence. Google identified physical threats in user content from Truth Social's Android app submission, while Truth Social's parent company, Trump Media & Technology Group, said it is working with Google to comply with policies without compromising free speech. The social media app is available on Apple's App Store and could bypass Google by offering direct downloads or using other app stores like Samsung's Galaxy Store.

feed.title: Trump's Truth Social barred from Google Play store over content moderation concerns
feed.summary: Google has barred Donald Trump's Truth Social app from its Play Store due to concerns over its content moderation policies, meaning 44% of U.S. smartphone users cannot download it. While Truth Social acknowledges and plans to address these issues, Trump Media CEO Devin Nunes claimed the approval was solely up to Google. The app is already available on Apple's App Store, and Android users can access it via the web, but its unavailability on Google Play adds to the challenges faced by the platform, which was created as a "free-speech" alternative to Twitter.

feed.title: iPhone 14 could support satellite communication if Apple can settle on a partner
feed.summary: Apple's upcoming iPhone 14 models have completed hardware testing for satellite communication, according to analyst Ming-Chi Kuo. While the technology is ready, Apple has yet to secure a business partner for the service. Kuo suggests Globalstar could be Apple's initial partner, as the need for satellite communication is growing due to natural disasters and geopolitical conflicts.

feed.title: Songwriters and Music Publishers to Be Paid More by Streaming Services
feed.summary: Music publishers and streaming services have reached an agreement to increase the rates paid to songwriters for their music played on digital platforms like Spotify. This deal, struck between the National Music Publishers Association and the Digital Media Association, means publishers will receive up to 15.35% of music streamers’ revenue, which will be phased in over a five-year period. The agreement was finalized just before a trial was set to begin in front of the Copyright Royalty Board.

feed.title: Truth Social: content moderation issue keeps Trump app off Android phones | Donald Trump
feed.summary: Donald Trump's social media platform, Truth Social, has not been approved for distribution on the Google Play Store due to insufficient content moderation, a Google spokesperson announced. Google cited concerns about violations of its policies, including those prohibiting physical threats and incitement to violence. This delay prevents Truth Social from accessing approximately 40% of the US smartphone market, a setback for the platform.

feed.title: SpaceX, T-Mobile race to put an end to cell phone dead zones with help from outer space
feed.summary: T-Mobile and SpaceX are collaborating to launch "Coverage Above and Beyond" service by late 2023, aiming to eliminate cellular dead zones across the continental U.S., Hawaii, parts of Alaska, and surrounding waters using SpaceX's Starlink satellites. The service will initially offer basic text and voice communications through existing smartphones, leveraging T-Mobile's 1900 MHz frequency. This initiative marks a significant step toward ubiquitous cellular coverage, with other companies like Apple and AST SpaceMobile also developing similar satellite-based phone connectivity solutions.

feed.title: Snap CEO to restructure company, cut 20% of workforce
feed.summary: Snap Inc. is restructuring its operations and laying off 20% of its workforce after experiencing its slowest-ever revenue growth in Q2 and a significant net loss. CEO Evan Spiegel stated the company will refocus on revenue and user growth, and augmented reality (AR) to ensure long-term success. This strategic shift includes discontinuing non-core projects like Snap Originals, Minis, Games, Pixy, Zenly, and Voisey, while also authorizing a $500 million stock buyback program.

feed.title: DAX-ETF statt MSCI-World-ETF? Was der Fokus auf deutsche Aktien wirklich bringt
feed.summary: This article examines whether a DAX-ETF is a viable alternative or supplement to an MSCI World-ETF for investors. The author argues that while German companies in the DAX have a long history, focusing solely on a DAX-ETF has historically yielded poorer returns compared to the more diversified MSCI World. Key reasons cited against a DAX-ETF include its historical underperformance, regional concentration, lack of tech companies, and limited number of constituent stocks.

feed.title: Arm sues Qualcomm, wants it to “destroy” Nuvia’s pre-acquisition CPU designs
feed.summary: Arm is suing Qualcomm over its $1.4 billion acquisition of Nuvia, claiming the purchase caused Nuvia to breach its Arm licenses. Arm alleges that Qualcomm and Nuvia continued to develop Arm-based technology under the terminated licenses without consent, demanding that they stop using and destroy the relevant Nuvia technology. Qualcomm denies these claims, asserting it has broad licensing rights covering its custom-designed CPUs.

feed.title: T-Mobile US makes bold eSIM push to poach new customers
feed.summary: T-Mobile US is leveraging eSIM technology to attract new customers by offering a free three-month trial of its network and an "Easy Switch" feature. This strategy allows non-customers with unlocked eSIM-compatible smartphones to experience T-Mobile's service, including 5G, with minimal effort. Analysts view this as an innovative move that prepares carriers for an eSIM-only future and highlights T-Mobile's confidence in its network performance.

feed.title: Arm sues Qualcomm, aiming to unwind Qualcomm's $1.4 bln Nuvia purchase
feed.summary: Arm Ltd. has sued Qualcomm Inc. and its recently acquired chip design firm Nuvia Inc. for breach of license agreements and trademark infringement, seeking to unwind Qualcomm's $1.4 billion Nuvia purchase. Arm is requesting an injunction that would compel Qualcomm to destroy designs developed under Nuvia’s license agreements with Arm, alleging its approval was needed before the transfer to Qualcomm. This lawsuit signifies a major rift between two key technology partners, as Qualcomm had acquired Nuvia to boost its custom computing core designs and challenge competitors in the PC market.

feed.title: Friday Q&A: iRhythm CTO Mark Day talks Zio Watch, Verily partnership, Big Tech in healthcare
feed.summary: iRhythm Technologies CTO Mark Day discusses the new Zio Watch, a diagnostic smartwatch for cardiac monitoring developed in partnership with Alphabet's Verily. Day emphasizes that the Zio Watch is a prescribed diagnostic tool, distinguishing it from consumer smartwatches like the Apple Watch, and addresses the strategy for clinical evidence, reimbursement, and market evaluation. He also shares his views on Big Tech's complementary role in raising awareness for conditions like atrial fibrillation.

feed.title: Tencent Music to work with Billboard in bringing Chinese artists to the global stage
feed.summary: Tencent Music Entertainment (TME) has partnered with Billboard to launch the "Chinese Music Gravity Project," aimed at promoting Chinese music and artists globally. This collaboration will integrate Billboard's global content onto TME's platforms and use TME's insights with Billboard's presence to enhance Chinese music's international impact. The partnership follows Billboard's recent launch of Billboard China and aligns with TME's ongoing efforts to bring Chinese music to a global audience, despite regulatory challenges in China's music streaming industry.

feed.title: Ryder System acquires Baton developing proprietary logistics technology
feed.summary: Ryder System, Inc. has acquired Baton, a San Francisco-based logistics technology startup, following an earlier investment through its venture capital fund, RyderVentures. This acquisition aims to enhance Ryder's supply chain and transportation solutions by integrating Baton's proprietary technology and talented development team. Baton's cofounders, Andrew Berberick and Nate Robert, will now lead innovation in customer-facing technologies for Ryder's supply chain and dedicated transportation businesses.

feed.title: Self-driving tech firm Aurora mulls sale to Apple or Microsoft - Bloomberg News
feed.summary: Aurora Innovation Inc CEO Chris Urmson is exploring strategic options for the self-driving tech company, including a potential sale to Apple or Microsoft, to navigate challenging market conditions. Other considerations include cost cuts, going private, spinning off assets, and acquiring smaller companies. The company's shares rose 15% on the news but have suffered significantly this year, and it recently delayed the delivery of its autonomous freight trucks.

feed.title: Herbalife Scores With Galaxy Deal
feed.summary: Herbalife Nutrition Ltd. has renewed its long-term partnership with the LA Galaxy in a multimillion-dollar deal, highlighting the growing value of sports sponsorships. This extension makes it the longest partnership in Major League Soccer history, with Herbalife continuing as the official jersey sponsor, presenting sponsor, and exclusive nutrition partner.
