The deal reflects the increasing recognition of sports sponsorships as an effective advertising strategy, especially with the rising popularity of MLS.

feed.title: 7 Growth Stocks That Could 10X by 2027
feed.summary: This article identifies seven growth stocks that have the potential to appreciate tenfold by 2027, despite some experiencing declines in 2022. The author selected these stocks based on strong analyst ratings and their performance in previous years, aiming to provide a diversified list from various MarketWatch categories. Each stock, including Nvidia, Dick's Sporting Goods, Perficient, Moderna, Apple, O'Reilly Automotive, and Cross Country Healthcare, is analyzed for its growth prospects, financial health, and analyst sentiment.

feed.title: Donald Trump’s Media Company Faces Setback As SPAC Seeks Approval To Extend Time To Complete Deal
feed.summary: Digital World Acquisition Corp. (DWAC), the SPAC set to merge with Donald Trump's TMTG, is struggling to gain enough shareholder support to extend its deadline to complete the deal. If 65% of shareholders don't approve the extension by Thursday, the SPAC may be forced to liquidate, hindering Trump Media and Technology Group's financial plans. The deal has been complicated by a federal probe and Truth Social's recent ban from the Google Play store due to content moderation violations.

feed.title: News | Back to the Office: Employers Push for a Post-Labor Day Return
feed.summary: Following Labor Day, employers across various sectors, including tech and finance, are intensifying efforts to bring employees back to the office, moving past the pandemic's impact on workplace norms. Companies are employing diverse strategies, from strict mandates to appealing new office amenities, to encourage hybrid or full-time in-person work. This shift occurs despite a strengthened employee preference for remote work and a tight labor market, highlighting a complex balancing act between company requirements and employee flexibility.

feed.title: Apple to appeal Brazil sales ban of iPhone without charger
feed.summary: Apple Inc. plans to appeal a Brazilian order that bans the sale of iPhones without a battery charger, issued after the Justice Ministry fined Apple and deemed the product incomplete. The ministry rejected Apple's environmental carbon reduction claims, stating there was no evidence to support them. Apple maintains customer awareness of charging options and has previously won court rulings on this issue in Brazil.

feed.title: Apple Unveils New iPhones & Watches
feed.summary: This article from the National Review announces Apple's fall product event, where the company unveiled new models of its iPhone and Apple Watch lineup. The content provided is minimal, serving primarily as a placeholder or headline for a more detailed report.

feed.title: Sales Per Square Foot: Retail Calculations & Metrics (2024)
feed.summary: This article explains the retail metric "sales per square foot," detailing its calculation and importance for brick-and-mortar stores. It covers how this metric helps measure store performance, inform merchandising decisions, analyze staff productivity, and identify sales trends over time. The article also discusses the limitations of sales per square foot, particularly in the age of omnichannel retail, and provides strategies to increase it, such as improving store layout, building loyalty programs, and leveraging online audiences.

feed.title: Sony Music Fully Abandons Operations in Russia Amid Ongoing Ukraine War
feed.summary: Sony Music has announced its complete withdrawal from Russia, six months after first suspending operations due to the invasion of Ukraine. The company cited the devastating humanitarian impact of the war and increasing sanctions as reasons for transferring its Russian entity and local roster to local management. Other major labels like Warner Music Group and Universal Music Group, along with various music-related organizations, have also suspended or ceased operations in Russia.

feed.title: Apple’s iPhone 14 launch sets off competing wireless promotions
feed.summary: The launch of Apple's iPhone 14 has ignited fierce promotional competition among major U.S. wireless carriers, including AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon. These promotions involve free phones, discounts, and bundled services like Apple One to attract and retain customers. The competitive landscape is impacting carrier subscriber growth and profit margins as they vie for market share.

feed.title: Union groups, investors seek worker rights review at Apple
feed.summary: Labor groups and investors have filed a shareholder proposal requesting Apple Inc. to review its worker rights practices, citing a potential "misalignment" between Apple's public commitments and alleged anti-union activities. This proposal comes amidst growing unionization efforts at Apple retail stores and builds on previous successful shareholder resolutions concerning civil rights audits and non-disclosure agreements.

feed.title: Vinci Brands Announces kate spade new york and Coach Branded Protective Cases for iPhone 14 Devices
feed.summary: Vinci Brands has launched new protective cases for the iPhone 14 series under their licensed brands, kate spade new york and Coach. These fashion-forward cases emphasize sustainability, crafted from recycled plastic with features like drop protection, scratch resistance, and antimicrobial properties. The collections offer a variety of designs, including MagSafe compatible options, appealing to consumers seeking both style and device protection.

feed.title: Netflix looks to control cloud computing costs with AWS - WSJ
feed.summary: Netflix is reportedly working to control its rising cloud computing costs with Amazon Web Services as part of a broader effort to reduce expenses. The streaming giant is also hiring more junior staff, reducing its real-estate footprint, and minimizing data storage globally following subscriber losses and increased competition. These measures aim to help Netflix manage costs and achieve its goal of 500 million global subscribers in the next three years.

feed.title: How to Buy NASDAQ Stocks
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to buy NASDAQ stocks, detailing what the NASDAQ is, who owns its shares, and the pros and cons of investing in them. It covers the steps to purchase NASDAQ stocks, from fixing a budget and choosing a broker to placing an order, and includes a brief history of the exchange. The article also mentions specific index funds and prominent companies listed on the NASDAQ.

feed.title: Apple lovers in some Asian countries to pay more for iPhone 14
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has maintained iPhone 14 prices in the United States but increased them in select Asian countries, such as Japan, due to local currency depreciation against the dollar. While Japan saw a significant price hike reflecting a 24% yen slump, China maintained its iPhone 14 pricing despite a 7% currency drop, although analysts anticipate weakening demand there due to COVID-19 lockdowns affecting consumer spending. The dollar's strength is identified as a major risk to Apple's earnings.

feed.title: CORSAIR K100 AIR wireless mechanical keyboard is shockingly thin
feed.summary: The CORSAIR K100 AIR is a new, shockingly thin wireless mechanical keyboard that combines premium mechanical keyswitches with a super slim design, measuring just 11mm at its slimmest point. It offers versatile connectivity options including Bluetooth, Slipstream Wireless, and wired USB-C with 8,000Hz hyper-polling for responsive gaming. The keyboard also features RGB backlighting, 8MB of onboard storage, and a long-lasting battery, and is expected to be available on October 8, 2022.

feed.title: Vodafone launches the Phone Buy-Back Guarantee with the release of the brand new Apple iPhone 14
feed.summary: Vodafone has introduced a new Phone Buy-Back Guarantee scheme alongside the launch of the Apple iPhone 14 series. This unique UK offer allows customers to sell their iPhone 14 back to Vodafone for a guaranteed price next year, with amounts up to £871 depending on the model, facilitating earlier upgrades. The scheme also complements Vodafone's trade-in program and aims to provide an affordable way for customers to access the latest technology.

feed.title: Apple to be largest user of Globalstar’s satellite network for iPhone messaging
feed.summary: Apple announced a partnership with Globalstar to provide satellite messaging services for its new iPhone 14 series, making Apple Globalstar’s largest customer. The "Emergency SOS" feature will allow iPhone users to send messages via satellite when out of terrestrial network coverage, with services starting in November in the U.S. and Canada. Globalstar revealed that Apple will fund 95% of the costs for a new generation of satellites and will allocate 85% of its network capacity to Apple's service.

feed.title: Apple asks appeals court to overturn $502 mln verdict in VPN patent case
feed.summary: Apple is asking a U.S. appeals court to overturn a $502 million jury verdict in a patent infringement case brought by VirnetX Inc concerning VPN technology. The dispute has lasted 12 years and involved multiple trials and appeals. Both companies acknowledge that the outcome could hinge on whether the Patent Trial and Appeal Board's rulings invalidating VirnetX's patents are upheld.

feed.title: Musk says SpaceX discussed iPhone satellite service with Apple
feed.summary: Elon Musk revealed that SpaceX has engaged in discussions with Apple Inc. regarding the integration of Starlink's satellite connectivity into iPhones. This announcement follows Apple's recent partnership with Globalstar Inc. for satellite services on its latest iPhone model, indicating a growing trend in offering satellite-based communication for smartphones. Musk emphasized that dedicated technology is crucial for optimal satellite-to-cell phone service.

feed.title: Here are the iPhone 14 deals from AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon as preorders begin
feed.summary: Preorders for Apple's new iPhone 14 lineup are set to begin on Friday, September 9th, at 8 a.m. Eastern time. The article announces the start of preorders for the newly released iPhone 14 models from Apple, highlighting camera improvements across all four new phones and the faster A16 processor in the Pro models, along with a redesigned top area. It serves as an announcement for consumers looking to purchase the latest iPhone models from carriers like AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon.

feed.title: Google, Apple facing anti-competitive complaint in Mexico
feed.summary: Apple and Google are facing an anti-competitive complaint in Mexico filed by former telecommunications chief Mony de Swaan Addati. The complaint alleges that both companies exploit their app store monopolies by forcing the use of their proprietary payment systems for in-app purchases, charging commissions of 15%-20%, and thereby inflating prices. De Swaan Addati hopes Mexico's telecommunications regulator (IFT) will investigate and address these practices.

feed.title: NetEase Games and Shueisha Games Announce unVEIL the world, a Tactical Hero Summoning RPG for iOS and Android
feed.summary: NetEase Games and Shueisha Games announced "unVEIL the world," a new tactical hero summoning RPG for iOS and Android, at Tokyo Game Show 2022. The game features an epic story centered around a gigantic tower, with character designs by Posuka Demizu, known for "The Promised Neverland." This collaboration aims to deliver an unparalleled gaming experience by combining Shueisha's content creation expertise with NetEase Games' development strengths.

feed.title: The Scholastic Art & Writing Awards Open Entries as the Program Celebrates 100 Years of Recognizing The Nation's Creative Teens
feed.summary: The Scholastic Art & Writing Awards have opened entries for their 100th annual program, inviting students in grades 7-12 from the U.S. and Canada to submit original works. In celebration of its centennial, the program, administered by the Alliance for Young Artists & Writers, announced nearly half a million dollars in scholarships, including increased portfolio awards and the inaugural Dav Pilkey Awards. These awards will recognize 12 high school seniors and their educators for outstanding portfolios, continuing the program's legacy of fostering young creative talent.

feed.title: Paychex Time by Paychex, Inc
feed.summary: Paychex Time is a mobile application for previously registered Paychex Flex users, allowing them to punch in and out quickly from their mobile devices. The app integrates with Paychex Flex Time or Flex Time and Labor Online subscriptions, offering features like a "Stay Logged In" option for quick access. It also includes an Apple Watch app for added convenience.

feed.title: Tractor Giant John Deere Eyes 10% of Revenue from Software Fees
feed.summary: John Deere aims for software fees to constitute 10% of its annual revenue by 2030, leveraging smart technology in its farm equipment. The company has invested in AI and autonomous capabilities through acquisitions, connecting 1.5 million machines to its cloud-based Operations Center for data collection. This strategic shift is driven by the high gross margins of farming software compared to equipment sales, though it faces concerns from farmers regarding fiscal independence and repair restrictions.

feed.title: Apple’s Latest Products and Services Are About Loyalty—to Apple
feed.summary: Apple recently unveiled its new iPhone 14 line, Apple Watch Ultra, and AirPods, with the overarching theme being to further integrate users into the Apple ecosystem. The company aims to enhance user loyalty through these product and service updates. The article also mentions a notable past debate between CEO Tim Cook and Steve Jobs.

feed.title: Can the West wean itself off Taiwan’s semiconductors?
feed.summary: The article discusses the West's reliance on Taiwan, particularly Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), for semiconductors and the efforts by the US and EU to onshore chip production due to geopolitical tensions with China. Despite significant investments like the US Chips Act and the European Chips Act, experts believe completely weaning off Taiwanese semiconductors would be costly and time-consuming, possibly creating new supply chain problems. The piece also highlights the complex nature of the global semiconductor supply chain, as US-made chips have been found in Russian weaponry despite sanctions.

feed.title: Plenty of Robinhood Stocks Found in These ETFs
feed.summary: The article discusses how many popular stocks among Robinhood users are also found in prominent ETFs like the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). Robinhood's newly introduced Robinhood Investor Index, which tracks the top 100 stocks in client portfolios, reveals that a significant portion consists of large-cap names and stocks in the communication services, consumer discretionary, and technology sectors, aligning with the holdings of QQQ and QQQM. This suggests Robinhood users may be more practical in their investment choices than their "meme stock" reputation implies.

feed.title: Graphjet Technology, the State-of-the-Art Graphene and Graphite Producer, Announces Membership Agreement with the MIT Industrial Liaison Program
feed.summary: Graphjet Technology Sdn. Bhd., a Malaysian graphene and graphite producer, has joined the Massachusetts Institute of Technology's Industrial Liaison Program (MIT ILP), becoming the first Malaysian company to do so. This membership will provide Graphjet with access to MIT's expertise, research, and educational opportunities, aiming to advance its technology development and strengthen its position in the industry. Through this collaboration, Graphjet seeks to connect with leading companies and talents to apply new technologies, particularly in areas like big data, automobiles, manufacturing, and healthcare.

feed.title: Tech stocks crushed in market selloff
feed.summary: The six largest U.S. tech companies collectively lost over $500 billion in value following an unexpectedly high August inflation report. The Nasdaq Composite experienced its steepest drop since June 2020, sinking 5.16%. Major companies like Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, and Nvidia saw significant declines, with the report potentially influencing further aggressive interest rate hikes from the Fed.

feed.title: Company created by two South Africans tops S&P 500 30-year performance
feed.summary: Monster Beverage Corporation, co-founded by two South African WITS graduates, has ranked as the top-performing stock in the S&P 500 over the last 30 years, achieving a staggering 213,088% total return. The company, initially Hansen Natural Corporation, was acquired in 1990 by Rodney Sacks and Hilton Schlosberg and saw significant growth after launching Monster Energy in 2002. This success story highlights the remarkable financial performance of the energy drink giant, even outperforming tech titans like Amazon, Apple, and Nvidia.

feed.title: The Only Stock Warren Buffett Has Bought in Each of the Last 2 Bear Markets
feed.summary: During recent bear markets, Warren Buffett has strategically purchased shares of numerous companies, primarily focusing on energy stocks like Chevron and Occidental Petroleum, and technology giants such as Apple and HP. However, the one constant investment across both the March 2020 coronavirus crash and the current bear market is Berkshire Hathaway itself. Buffett has overseen significant share repurchases of his own company, indicating his strong belief in its intrinsic value and long-term prospects.

feed.title: Microsoft patches a new zero-day affecting all versions of Windows
feed.summary: Microsoft has issued security patches for a zero-day vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2022-37969, impacting all supported Windows versions, including Windows 11 and earlier, and Windows Server. This elevation of privilege flaw, which allows attackers to gain system privileges, has been exploited in real-world attacks. Additionally, Microsoft addressed another zero-day, CVE-2022-23960, a cache speculation vulnerability affecting Windows 11 for ARM-based systems, as part of its monthly Patch Tuesday release.

feed.title: Racing Archives
feed.summary: The Racing Archives features recent NASCAR news, including Sheldon Creed's first career O'Reilly Series win and Kyle Busch's third consecutive Atlanta victory in the Craftsman Truck Series. It also highlights Colin Braun's return to NASCAR after 15 years and other updates from the racing world.

feed.title: Apple Watch Ultra vs Garmin Fenix 7: which rugged wearable is best?
feed.summary: This article compares the Apple Watch Ultra and Garmin Fenix 7, two rugged smartwatches designed for outdoor enthusiasts. It delves into their differences in pricing, tech specs, design, display, health and fitness tracking, software, and battery life, aiming to determine which device is superior for adventurers. While the Apple Watch Ultra offers a smarter experience with a vibrant OLED screen and dive capabilities, the Garmin Fenix 7 excels in battery life and extensive activity-specific metrics, making the choice dependent on individual priorities.

feed.title: Emerge EMPWR Unified Sustainable Equity ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed profile of the Emerge EMPWR Unified Sustainable Equity ETF (EMPW), noting it is no longer active. It covers key financial data, including its expense ratio, inception date, AUM, and historical trading data. The profile also lists its top holdings, concentration analysis, performance metrics, geographic and sector exposures, and technical indicators, offering a comprehensive overview for investors.

feed.title: OTT Platform DISNEY+ HOTSTAR now supports Dolby Atmos on compatible TVs, AVRs, soundbars, Android smartphones and iOS devices
feed.summary: Disney+ Hotstar has expanded its audio support to include Dolby Atmos for its users, delivering a multidimensional, spatial sound experience on compatible devices. This new feature, complementing existing Dolby Vision support, aims to immerse users more deeply in their favorite movies and shows. A wide range of titles, including Hotstar Specials and Multiplex content, are now available with Dolby Atmos.

feed.title: Strive 500 ETF (STRV) Price, Holdings, & News
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Strive 500 ETF (STRV), including its current price, holdings, and news. It outlines the ETF's investment strategy, expense ratio, top holdings like NVIDIA and Apple, and recent news headlines related to its performance and capital attraction. The piece serves as a comprehensive resource for potential investors interested in the STRV ETF.

feed.title: The Business of Formula 1
feed.summary: Formula 1 is a global multi-billion dollar sport, attracting sponsorships from major brands and boasting a vast audience. This article explores F1's business model, including its ownership by Liberty Media, key revenue streams from hosting fees, broadcasting, and sponsorships, and highlights its highest-earning drivers and team owners. It delves into the financial powerhouses and individual wealth within the world of F1.

feed.title: First It Was Health Monitoring Devices on Wrists, Now It’s Health Monitoring on Fingers
feed.summary: The article discusses the emergence of smart rings for health monitoring, highlighting Movano, Inc.'s new Movano Ring and the existing Oura Ring. These devices offer miniaturized diagnostic capabilities, tracking various health metrics like heart rate, temperature, and sleep. Movano is seeking FDA clearance and aims to eventually add cuffless blood pressure and blood glucose monitoring.

feed.title: New iPhones have Qualcomm satellite modem, new Apple radio chips
feed.summary: Apple's new iPhone 14 models incorporate a Qualcomm X65 modem chip, which enables satellite connectivity for emergency messaging. While the Qualcomm chip provides the core satellite communication capability, Apple clarified that the iPhone 14 also contains proprietary custom-designed radio frequency components and software specifically developed by Apple for its Emergency SOS via satellite feature. This new feature allows users to send emergency messages when outside of WiFi or cellular service, utilizing Globalstar's satellite network capacity.

feed.title: Why EVs need a 12V battery (Advance Auto Parts interview)
feed.summary: Electric vehicles (EVs) require a 12V battery in addition to their larger lithium-ion battery, similar to internal combustion engine vehicles. This article explores the critical role of the 12V battery in EVs, discussing its function and how it differs from 12V batteries in traditional cars. Allison Lentz Bubar, SVP of marketing & DIT strategy at Advance Auto Parts, provides insights into these aspects in an interview.

feed.title: Apple Will Hike App Store Prices from Europe to Asia Next Month
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is increasing App Store prices for apps and in-app purchases across Europe and Asia, effective October 5. This move is primarily to protect its margins due to the strong US dollar against other major currencies. The price hikes will affect countries using the euro, as well as Sweden, Japan, South Korea, Chile, Egypt, Malaysia, Pakistan, and Vietnam, with Vietnam's increase also tied to new local tax regulations.

feed.title: S&P 500 Buybacks Decline 21.8% as Financials Pull Back; 12-Months Buybacks Pass $1 Trillion for the First Time
feed.summary: S&P 500 stock buybacks decreased by 21.8% in Q2 2022 to $219.6 billion from Q1's record $281.0 billion, primarily due to a pullback from Financials and Health Care sectors. Despite the quarterly decline, the 12-month expenditure ending June 2022 surpassed $1 trillion for the first time, reaching $1.005 trillion, a 65.0% increase from the prior year. An anticipated 1% excise tax on net buybacks in 2023 is not expected to significantly impact overall buyback activity in 2023, though some 2023 purchases may be accelerated into Q4 2022.

feed.title: Spotify Offers Audiobook Service With 300,000 Titles
authors: Anne Steele and Jeffrey A. Trachtenberg
feed.summary: Spotify is expanding its services beyond music and podcasts by launching an audiobook offering with over 300,000 titles. Users will be able to purchase audiobooks on a pay-per-download basis, similar to other major audiobook providers. Initially, recommendations will be editorially curated, with algorithmic recommendations based on user listening habits planned for the future.

feed.title: Strive launches Strive 500 ETF
feed.summary: Strive Asset Management has introduced its second index fund, the Strive 500 ETF (NYSE: STRV), designed to mirror the Solactive GBS United States 500 Index. The ETF aims to provide diversified large-cap exposure to US corporations and seeks to unlock investor value through pro-fiduciary shareholder engagement, moving companies to prioritize value maximization over other agendas. Strive emphasizes its commitment to clients' financial interests, independent of social or political objectives.

feed.title: Apple Pay llegaría a fines de octubre a Chile
feed.summary: Apple Pay is expected to launch in Chile by the end of October, according to financial industry executives and various indications. Several banks, including Banco de Chile, Bci, Banco Falabella, Itaú, BICE, Scotiabank, and Santander, are preparing for its arrival. The delay has been attributed to commercial differences, particularly Apple's demand for a share of transaction commissions, and technical implementation challenges related to Chile's evolving payment system and POS terminal requirements.

feed.title: Lumentum and Coherent carve up VCSEL market
feed.summary: Lumentum and Coherent now dominate the market for VCSELs, collectively holding 80% market share according to a Yole Intelligence report. Their dominance is largely attributed to close ties with Apple and strategic acquisitions, as VCSEL technology, initially expected to proliferate widely, has primarily seen sustained adoption in Apple iPhones. The VCSEL market is projected to reach $4 billion by 2027, driven by datacom and growing automotive applications.

feed.title: Western Union’s transformation: From traditional wires to digital wallets in Latin America
feed.summary: Western Union is expanding its digital wallet services into Latin America, focusing initially on Brazil, a strategically important market. The company acquired Brazilian digital wallet provider Te Enviei to accelerate its market penetration and offer an integrated digital banking and money transfer solution. This move is part of Western Union's broader strategy to modernize its operations and shift from a transactional business to a customer-centric, relationship-based approach in response to global digital financial trends.

feed.title: Herbalife Nutrition Survey Reveals Top Ten Modern Day Entrepreneurs and What Traits are Needed to Join the List
feed.summary: A Herbalife Nutrition survey identified Bill Gates, Jeff Bezos, and Steve Jobs as the top three most influential modern entrepreneurs, followed by Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg. The study, involving 2,750 respondents across the U.S., Mexico, and Israel, also explored traits essential for entrepreneurial success, highlighting creativity and intelligence as most important. Herbalife Nutrition emphasized the ongoing strength of the entrepreneurial spirit and the company's commitment to fostering it.
